title: LME aluminium cash prices soar beyond $3,300/t while inventories slide downhill
summary: LME aluminium cash bid and offer prices rose 1.14%, with the bid price shifting from USD 3,294 per tonne on March 25 to USD 3,331.5 per tonne. Inventories continue to decline amid the price surge.

title: Aluminum Prices on a Sustained Rise – Industry Trends and Best Practices
summary: Aluminum prices are on a sustained upward trajectory due to tightening supply constraints, structural demand expansion, systemic cost inflation, and geopolitical volatility with low inventory levels. Accelerated copper-to-aluminum substitution is projected to add 1.2 million tons of demand in 2026. These drivers are expected to intensify in 2026, pushing prices higher.

title: LME Aluminium Price Surge Hits $3,269/Tonne in March 2026
summary: LME aluminium cash offer price reached $3,269 per tonne on March 3, 2026, amid inventory drawdowns and supply constraints from energy costs, environmental regulations, and electrification demand. The forward curve shows immediate tightness with a $132 per tonne discount to December 2027 futures, indicating sustained elevated pricing.

title: Aluminum Prices Rise Above $3600/Ton in Early March 2026
summary: Aluminum prices surged above $3,600 per ton since late February due to tighter supply from energy costs and environmental regulations, alongside robust demand from infrastructure and new energy sectors. Prices rose about $220 per ton in ten days, over 6%, impacting buyers in machining, automotive, and aerospace. Analysts expect short-term strength if demand from EVs and renewables continues.

title: Aluminum Shortages Coming Soon || Peter Zeihan
summary: Peter Zeihan warns that aluminum production in the Persian Gulf is shutting down due to Iranian strikes on gas fields, pipelines, and power plants, affecting smelters reliant on cheap natural gas. Facilities in the region, including some of the world's largest, are operating at less than half capacity and may fully shut down within weeks, accounting for about 9% of global primary aluminum. This shortage, combined with existing US tariffs, will drive up primary aluminum prices.

title: Aluminium prices expected to rise with prolonged war set to hit Gulf producers
summary: Aluminum prices have reached a near four-year high due to supply disruptions from regional war, including closure of the Strait of Hormuz, affecting Middle East exporters. Bahrain's Alba declared force majeure, and Qatar’s Qatalum shut down production due to gas shortages, with analysts predicting prices could hit $3,700-$4,000 per tonne if disruptions persist amid a forecasted 2026 market deficit. Prices rose to $3,544 a tonne before slightly falling after US President Trump's comments on the war potentially ending soon.

title: IBAAS–2026 to Spotlight Innovation, Sustainability and Digital Transformation in the Global Aluminium Industry
summary: IBAAS 2026 will unite global aluminium experts in India, focusing on bauxite innovation, recycling, green aluminium, AI, and sustainable practices. The event emphasizes digital transformation and sustainability in the aluminium sector.

title: ALUMINIUM 2026 Launches New Industry Innovation Awards
summary: ALUMINIUM 2026 introduces Innovation Awards celebrating breakthroughs in sustainable manufacturing, technology, and smart automation. Categories include Sustainable & Low-Carbon Manufacturing, Technology & Product Innovation, and Smart Manufacturing, with submissions open to exhibitors from May.

title: Aluminum at the Centre of Global Industrial Transformation AICE 2026
summary: AICE 2026 addresses regulatory pressures and sustainability commitments accelerating investments in scrap collection, sorting, and remelting technologies for aluminium. The event facilitates discussion on capacity constraints, carbon reduction, and closed-loop systems for construction, automotive, and packaging.

title: Recycled AL & Sustainability update: Plant fire, low-carbon cable trials, recycling gains and major global expansions
summary: Recent developments in recycled aluminium include fire disruptions at plants, low-carbon cable trials, recycling gains, and strategic global expansions. These updates highlight ongoing efforts to shape a sustainable aluminium industry.

title: Steel rebar futures fall below CNY 3,130 per ton amid Tangshan pollution emergency
summary: Steel rebar futures hit a two-week low as China's steelmaking hub Tangshan activated emergency pollution response measures, raising concerns about potential output curbs. Chinese steel production declined 3.6% to 76.1 million tons in February, while global output fell 2.2% to 141.8 million tons. Steel prices rose 0.13% to 3,126 CNY/T on March 27, though they remain 2.22% lower than a year ago.

title: Commodity Price Watch: March 2026
summary: Steel prices are higher in the US and Europe while remaining largely flat in Asia due to oversupply relative to consumption. Middle East conflict-related disruptions to natural gas supply are driving higher electricity costs that partially feed into steel pricing, though weak global demand prevents full cost pass-through. Mainland Chinese prices are expected to remain mostly flat as output exceeds consumption.

title: MOC: Average high-speed wire rod price in China up 0.2% in Mar 16-22, 2026
summary: Chinese high-speed wire rod prices increased 0.2% during the week of March 16-22, 2026, reflecting modest upward movement in the domestic market.

title: Global Crude Steel Output Falls 2.2% Year-on-Year in February 2026
summary: Global crude steel production declined 2.2% year-on-year to 141.8 million tonnes in February 2026 across 69 countries reporting to the World Steel Association. Regional performance was mixed, with China experiencing a 3.6% decline despite remaining the dominant producer, while India and the United States posted notable growth of 9.7% and 4.9% respectively. Russia and other CIS nations plus Ukraine reported a sharp 10.5% decline.

title: European Steel Industry News - Weekly Podcast
summary: Istanbul's EUROMETAL Steel Day and YİSAD Flat Steel Conference highlighted tighter EU trade measures, rising CBAM costs, and mounting pressure from Chinese competition. EUROFER flagged record global overcapacity, while Swedish steelmaker SSAB continues its fossil-free transition with a new electric arc furnace at Oxallosund planned for early 2027.

title: Iranian Steel Plants Damaged: Supply Chain Crisis Unfolds
summary: Iranian steel plants with significant projected annual capacity have been damaged, creating supply chain disruptions. The incident impacts global steel market dynamics during a period of already-strained capacity.

title: Copper prices drop amid global growth fears, plump stocks
summary: Copper prices on the London Metal Exchange fell 9.4% to $12,081.74 per metric ton since late February, driven by fears of weakened economic growth from high energy prices amid the US-Israel war with Iran. Global copper inventories have surpassed 1 million metric tons for the first time since 2003, increasing supply availability. Analysts from BMO Capital Markets downgraded their near-term copper outlook due to the disrupted macro environment.

title: Copper - Price - Chart - Historical Data - News
summary: Copper prices fluctuated around 5.44-5.47 USD per pound on March 27, down slightly daily but poised for a weekly gain amid hopes for a diplomatic resolution to the US-Iran conflict. President Trump extended a deadline for striking Iranian infrastructure to allow negotiations, with Iran permitting oil tankers through the Strait of Hormuz. Over the past month, prices fell about 7%, though still higher year-over-year.

title: Copper Drops $0.19 | Scrap Metal Prices Mixed — March 19, 2026
summary: Copper scrap prices dropped $0.19 per pound across major grades, with Comex copper settling at $5.48 per pound, down 4.9%, and LME 3-month copper at $12,395.50 per ton, down 2.97%. The declines align with broader market pressures from a stronger US dollar, rising energy costs, and geopolitical disruptions affecting industrial demand.

title: Copper futures reverse gains amid geopolitical tension
summary: Copper futures erased recent gains due to geopolitical tensions, including the closure of the Strait of Hormuz delaying copper concentrate shipments and tightening the market. Despite strong demand from data center buildouts estimated at 475,000 metric tons in 2026, rising energy costs and slowing industrial activity pose risks. Analyst Iaccino discusses the balance between supply shocks and declining business confidence.

title: Copper Prices Surge Above $13,000: Best Copper Stocks to Watch in 2026
summary: Copper prices have surged above $13,000 amid projections of growing demand to 42 million metric tons by 2040 and supply shortfalls, including J.P. Morgan's forecast of a 330,000 metric ton refined copper deficit in 2026. Freeport-McMoRan expects production ramp-up at Grasberg in Q2 2026 with 85% restoration by year-end, while Teck Resources guides 455,000-530,000 tonnes for 2026 from Quebrada Blanca Phase 2. Tech firms like Amazon are securing low-carbon copper supplies for AI infrastructure.

title: Glencore Copper Output Forecast Decreases for 2026
summary: Glencore lowered its 2026 copper output forecast to 840,000 tonnes from 930,000 tonnes due to ongoing production challenges at the Collahuasi mine in Chile. Current year output is expected at 850,000 tonnes, with rebound to 930,000 tonnes in 2027 and 1 million tonnes by 2028. Long-term, output could reach 1.6 million tonnes by 2035 through African mines, Alumbrera restart, and Latin American projects.
